final_text:
How ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini tend to handle the same underspecified brief — "write me a landing page pitch, make it punchy," no product/audience/tone given:

ChatGPT usually just picks reasonable defaults and writes the pitch, often without flagging what it assumed.
Claude is more likely to either ask 1-2 clarifying questions first, or write the pitch and explicitly list its assumptions alongside it.
Gemini's behavior swings more with how the prompt is worded — the same ask phrased slightly differently can get you an assumptions-flagged draft or a request for more detail.

If you're pasting in a real client brief at 11pm with no time to answer follow-up questions, the model that tells you what it assumed — instead of silently guessing — is the one that saves you a redo the next morning.

Which of these matches what you've actually seen?
